raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes sharpening the thesis, uncertainty distinction, and contribution framing above the sentence level.","R1.2":"A substantive improvement pass should check whether the argument from VQA ambiguity to ViDA-Q's design and evaluation follows coherently.","R1.3":"The draft makes performance and ablation claims, so improvement requires ensuring those claims are aligned with the reported results.","R1.5":"The draft's method, implementation details, baselines, and results may need section-level ordering and narrative strengthening.","R1.6":"The open-ended request to improve a draft makes sentence-level clarity, flow, concision, and register load-bearing.","RX.1":"Any improvement must preserve and disclose uncertainty around unsupported empirical, novelty, or evaluation claims.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong claims about prior work and gains, so avoiding overclaiming is an always-active responsibility.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within improving the draft rather than inventing results or performing unrelated review tasks.","RX.4":"The projection must account for avoiding downstream harm from misleading scientific claims or unsupported system capabilities.","RX.5":"Changes or judgments should remain traceable to the provided artifact and not rely on unstated sources."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes sharpening the thesis, uncertainty distinction, and contribution framing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: A substantive improvement pass should check whether the argument from VQA ambiguity to ViDA-Q's design and evaluation follows coherently.
rationale.R1.3: The draft makes performance and ablation claims, so improvement requires ensuring those claims are aligned with the reported results.
rationale.R1.5: The draft's method, implementation details, baselines, and results may need section-level ordering and narrative strengthening.
rationale.R1.6: The open-ended request to improve a draft makes sentence-level clarity, flow, concision, and register load-bearing.
rationale.RX.1: Any improvement must preserve and disclose uncertainty around unsupported empirical, novelty, or evaluation claims.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong claims about prior work and gains, so avoiding overclaiming is an always-active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within improving the draft rather than inventing results or performing unrelated review tasks.
rationale.RX.4: The projection must account for avoiding downstream harm from misleading scientific claims or unsupported system capabilities.
rationale.RX.5: Changes or judgments should remain traceable to the provided artifact and not rely on unstated sources.
